_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Configurable per-project scope-overlap gate with block/allow policy

steps.description: scope_overlap.py: new (block_patterns, allow_patterns) kw-only params + per-conflicting-glob allow filter; drop hardcoded is_test_path strip. project_registry.py: parse + validate overlap_gate block, expose on ProjectConfig with synthesized default (block=['**/*'], allow=test patterns). batch_manager.py: read policy, pass to find_blocking_items, emit item_overlap_allowed_by_policy event when default-strict would have held but policy released. TDD unit tests for both modules.

Integration test: two projects across two batches with overlapping source-file paths; one strict (default), one permissive on docs/**; assert BatchItem.status transitions and DaemonEvent rows (item_held_for_scope + item_overlap_allowed_by_policy). Real-scenario mirror of CR-00057 vs I-00087/I-00088.

dashboard/routers/batches.py: extend _held_reasons_for_items (or sibling helper) to also query item_overlap_allowed_by_policy events; return record indicating held vs allowed with matched patterns. Templates: new info-tone pill in batch_items_rows.html (and queue/batch_detail partials that share the snippet) with tooltip listing matched allow patterns.

Update docs/IW_AI_Core_Daemon_Design.md (new overlap_gate keys, decision tree, observability events, guidance on pairing with scope_gate_enabled). Brief mention in docs/IW_AI_Core_Architecture.md. Add explicit overlap_gate block in this repo's .iw-orch.json equivalent to the synthesized default. Update batches/queue/batch_detail help partials with a one-liner on the new pill.

Per-agent review of S01..S04 — allow-precedence correctness, default-equivalence with old is_test_path strip, event de-duplication semantics, dashboard pill accessibility, doc/code consistency, scope discipline

Cross-agent review of end-to-end policy round-trip (json → registry → ProjectConfig → batch_manager → scope_overlap → DaemonEvent → router → template), default-preservation invariant, event audit-trail completeness, no regression of F-00076 contract tests
QV: Browser verification — held-reason pill, allowed-by-policy pill, default-config no regression on existing batch/queue rendering
Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill
